title: Implement actual web proxy functionality

requirements.text: Upgrade the backend `proxyUrl` function to fully proxy arbitrary HTTP/HTTPS URLs via ICP HTTP outcalls: fetch the target URL, return the full response body (HTML, CSS, JS, images as base64), status code, and content-type header so the frontend can render actual remote web content.

quotes: make this website an actual proxy

acceptanceCriteria: Calling `proxyUrl` with a valid HTTPS URL returns the full response body and a 200-equivalent status.
The returned content-type header is preserved from the upstream response.
Binary resources (images) are returned as base64-encoded data.
Errors from the upstream server are propagated back with the appropriate status code.

requirements.text: Update `useProxyRequest` in `frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts` to pass the full response body and content-type returned by the upgraded backend actor to the caller, supporting HTML, CSS, JS, and image content types.

quotes: make this website an actual proxy

acceptanceCriteria: The mutation resolves with `{ body: string, contentType: string, statusCode: number }`.
HTML responses are passed through without modification.
Non-HTML binary content (images) is decoded from base64 and surfaced as a data URL.

requirements.text: Update `frontend/src/components/ProxyContent.tsx` to render the full proxied HTML response in the iframe using a `srcdoc` attribute (or a Blob URL) instead of a plain `src`, so actual remote page content is displayed. Rewrite relative URLs in the proxied HTML (links, images, scripts, stylesheets) to route back through the proxy via the `onNavigate` callback, preserving navigation within the proxy.

quotes: make this website an actual proxy

acceptanceCriteria: The proxied HTML is rendered inside the iframe using `srcdoc` or a Blob URL.
Relative URLs in the HTML (href, src, action attributes) are rewritten to resolve through the proxy.
Clicking links inside the proxied iframe triggers `onNavigate` with the resolved absolute URL.
Form submissions inside the iframe are intercepted and routed through `onNavigate`.
The address bar reflects the currently proxied URL.

requirements.text: Update `frontend/src/components/BrowserLayout.tsx` to handle loading states and errors during proxy requests, showing a spinner in the tab bar while the proxy fetch is in progress and an inline error message in the content area if the proxy request fails.

quotes: make this website an actual proxy

acceptanceCriteria: A loading indicator is visible in the active tab while a proxy request is pending.
If the proxy request fails, an error message is shown in the content area with the failed URL and a retry button.
Successful proxy responses clear any previous error state.

constraints: All backend logic must remain in the single actor in backend/main.mo.
Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
Proxy requests must go through the ICP HTTP outcalls mechanism — no external proxy servers or third-party relay services.
nonGoals: Adding user authentication or login-gating to the proxy.
Caching proxied responses in the backend.
Supporting POST/PUT/DELETE methods in the proxy (GET only for now).
